lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਂਈਂ ਮੇਰੈ ਚੰਗਾ ਕੀਤਾ ਨਾਹੀ ਤ ਹੰ ਭੀ ਦਝਾਂ ਆਹਿ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: saaneen merai changaa keetaa naahee ta han bhee dajhaan aaeh |3|
lines.english: My Lord did well to warn me about it; otherwise, I would have been burnt as well. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਿਝੁ ਨ ਬੁਝੈ ਕਿਝੁ ਨ ਸੁਝੈ ਦੁਨੀਆ ਗੁਝੀ ਭਾਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: kijh na bujhai kijh na sujhai duneea gujhee bhaeh |
lines.english: I know nothing; I understand nothing. The world is a smouldering fire.

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਜੇ ਜਾਣਾ ਤਿਲ ਥੋੜੜੇ ਸੰਮਲਿ ਬੁਕੁ ਭਰੀ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa je jaanaa til thorrarre samal buk bharee |
lines.english: Fareed, if I had known that I had so few sesame seeds, I would have been more careful with them in my hands.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੇ ਜਾਣਾ ਸਹੁ ਨੰਢੜਾ ਤਾਂ ਥੋੜਾ ਮਾਣੁ ਕਰੀ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: je jaanaa sahu nandtarraa taan thorraa maan karee |4|
lines.english: If I had known that my Husband Lord was so young and innocent, I would not have been so arrogant. ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੇ ਜਾਣਾ ਲੜੁ ਛਿਜਣਾ ਪੀਡੀ ਪਾਈਂ ਗੰਢਿ ॥
lines.translit: je jaanaa larr chhijanaa peeddee paaeen gandt |
lines.english: If I had known that my robe would come loose, I would have tied a tighter knot.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੈ ਜੇਵਡੁ ਮੈ ਨਾਹਿ ਕੋ ਸਭੁ ਜਗੁ ਡਿਠਾ ਹੰਢਿ ॥੫॥
lines.translit: tai jevadd mai naeh ko sabh jag dditthaa handt |5|
lines.english: I have found none as great as You, Lord; I have looked and searched throughout the world. ||5||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਜੇ ਤੂ ਅਕਲਿ ਲਤੀਫੁ ਕਾਲੇ ਲਿਖੁ ਨ ਲੇਖ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa je too akal lateef kaale likh na lekh |
lines.english: Fareed, if you have a keen understanding, then do not write black marks against anyone else.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪਨੜੇ ਗਿਰੀਵਾਨ ਮਹਿ ਸਿਰੁ ਨਂ‍ੀਵਾਂ ਕਰਿ ਦੇਖੁ ॥੬॥
lines.translit: aapanarre gireevaan meh sir naneevaan kar dekh |6|
lines.english: Look underneath your own collar instead. ||6||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਜੋ ਤੈ ਮਾਰਨਿ ਮੁਕੀਆਂ ਤਿਨੑਾ ਨ ਮਾਰੇ ਘੁੰਮਿ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa jo tai maaran mukeean tinaa na maare ghunm |
lines.english: Fareed, do not turn around and strike those who strike you with their fists.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪਨੜੈ ਘਰਿ ਜਾਈਐ ਪੈਰ ਤਿਨੑਾ ਦੇ ਚੁੰਮਿ ॥੭॥
lines.translit: aapanarrai ghar jaaeeai pair tinaa de chunm |7|
lines.english: Kiss their feet, and return to your own home. ||7||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਜਾਂ ਤਉ ਖਟਣ ਵੇਲ ਤਾਂ ਤੂ ਰਤਾ ਦੁਨੀ ਸਿਉ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa jaan tau khattan vel taan too rataa dunee siau |
lines.english: Fareed, when there was time for you to earn good karma, you were in love with the world instead.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਰਗ ਸਵਾਈ ਨੀਹਿ ਜਾਂ ਭਰਿਆ ਤਾਂ ਲਦਿਆ ॥੮॥
lines.translit: marag savaaee neehi jaan bhariaa taan ladiaa |8|
lines.english: Now, death has a strong foothold; when the load is full, it is taken away. ||8||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਗਹੁ ਨੇੜਾ ਆਇਆ ਪਿਛਾ ਰਹਿਆ ਦੂਰਿ ॥੯॥
lines.translit: agahu nerraa aaeaa pichhaa rahiaa door |9|
lines.english: That which is coming is near, and the past is left far behind. ||9||

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੇਖੁ ਫਰੀਦਾ ਜੁ ਥੀਆ ਦਾੜੀ ਹੋਈ ਭੂਰ ॥
lines.translit: dekh fareedaa ju theea daarree hoee bhoor |
lines.english: See, Fareed, what has happened: your beard has become grey.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਂਈ ਬਾਝਹੁ ਆਪਣੇ ਵੇਦਣ ਕਹੀਐ ਕਿਸੁ ॥੧੦॥
lines.translit: saanee baajhahu aapane vedan kaheeai kis |10|
lines.english: Without my Lord, who can I tell of my sorrow? ||10||

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੇਖੁ ਫਰੀਦਾ ਜਿ ਥੀਆ ਸਕਰ ਹੋਈ ਵਿਸੁ ॥
lines.translit: dekh fareedaa ji theea sakar hoee vis |
lines.english: See, Fareed, what has happened: sugar has become poison.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਖ ਪਕੰਦੀ ਆਈਆ ਹੋਰ ਕਰੇਂਦੀ ਵੰਨ ॥੧੧॥
lines.translit: saakh pakandee aaeea hor karendee van |11|
lines.english: The body's crop has become ripe and turned color. ||11||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਅਖੀ ਦੇਖਿ ਪਤੀਣੀਆਂ ਸੁਣਿ ਸੁਣਿ ਰੀਣੇ ਕੰਨ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa akhee dekh pateeneean sun sun reene kan |
lines.english: Fareed, my eyes have become weak, and my ears have become hard of hearing.

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਕਾਲਂ‍ੀ ਜਿਨੀ ਨ ਰਾਵਿਆ ਧਉਲੀ ਰਾਵੈ ਕੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa kaalanee jinee na raaviaa dhaulee raavai koe |
lines.english: Fareed, those who did not enjoy their Spouse when their hair was black - hardly any of them enjoy Him when their hair turns grey.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਰਿ ਸਾਂਈ ਸਿਉ ਪਿਰਹੜੀ ਰੰਗੁ ਨਵੇਲਾ ਹੋਇ ॥੧੨॥
lines.translit: kar saanee siau piraharree rang navelaa hoe |12|
lines.english: So be in love with the Lord, so that your color may ever be new. ||12||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਕਾਲੀ ਧਉਲੀ ਸਾਹਿਬੁ ਸਦਾ ਹੈ ਜੇ ਕੋ ਚਿਤਿ ਕਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa kaalee dhaulee saahib sadaa hai je ko chit kare |
lines.english: Fareed, whether one's hair is black or grey, our Lord and Master is always here if one remembers Him.

lines.translit: mahalaa 3 |
lines.english: Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪਣਾ ਲਾਇਆ ਪਿਰਮੁ ਨ ਲਗਈ ਜੇ ਲੋਚੈ ਸਭੁ ਕੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: aapanaa laaeaa piram na lagee je lochai sabh koe |
lines.english: This loving devotion to the Lord does not come by one's own efforts, even though all may long for it.

lines.gurmukhi: ਏਹੁ ਪਿਰਮੁ ਪਿਆਲਾ ਖਸਮ ਕਾ ਜੈ ਭਾਵੈ ਤੈ ਦੇਇ ॥੧੩॥
lines.translit: ehu piram piaalaa khasam kaa jai bhaavai tai dee |13|
lines.english: This cup of loving devotion belongs to our Lord and Master; He gives it to whomever He likes. ||13||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਜਿਨੑ ਲੋਇਣ ਜਗੁ ਮੋਹਿਆ ਸੇ ਲੋਇਣ ਮੈ ਡਿਠੁ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa jina loein jag mohiaa se loein mai dditth |
lines.english: Fareed, those eyes which have enticed the world - I have seen those eyes.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਜਲ ਰੇਖ ਨ ਸਹਦਿਆ ਸੇ ਪੰਖੀ ਸੂਇ ਬਹਿਠੁ ॥੧੪॥
lines.translit: kajal rekh na sahadiaa se pankhee sooe bahitth |14|
lines.english: Once, they could not endure even a bit of mascara; now, the birds hatch their young in them! ||14||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਕੂਕੇਦਿਆ ਚਾਂਗੇਦਿਆ ਮਤੀ ਦੇਦਿਆ ਨਿਤ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa kookediaa chaangediaa matee dediaa nit |
lines.english: Fareed, they shouted and yelled, and constantly gave good advice.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਸੈਤਾਨਿ ਵੰਞਾਇਆ ਸੇ ਕਿਤ ਫੇਰਹਿ ਚਿਤ ॥੧੫॥
lines.translit: jo saitaan vanyaaeaa se kit fereh chit |15|
lines.english: But those whom the devil has spoiled - how can they turn their consciousness towards God? ||15||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੇ ਸਾਂਈ ਲੋੜਹਿ ਸਭੁ ॥
lines.translit: je saanee lorreh sabh |
lines.english: if you long for the Lord of all.

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਥੀਉ ਪਵਾਹੀ ਦਭੁ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa theeo pavaahee dabh |
lines.english: Fareed, become the grass on the path,

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਖਾਕੁ ਨ ਨਿੰਦੀਐ ਖਾਕੂ ਜੇਡੁ ਨ ਕੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa khaak na nindeeai khaakoo jedd na koe |
lines.english: Fareed, do not slander the dust; noting is as great as dust.

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਕੁ ਛਿਜਹਿ ਬਿਆ ਲਤਾੜੀਅਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: eik chhijeh biaa lataarreeeh |
lines.english: One will cut you down, and another will trample you underfoot;

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਾਂ ਸਾਈ ਦੈ ਦਰਿ ਵਾੜੀਅਹਿ ॥੧੬॥
lines.translit: taan saaee dai dar vaarreeeh |16|
lines.english: then, you shall enter the Court of the Lord. ||16||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਜਾ ਲਬੁ ਤਾ ਨੇਹੁ ਕਿਆ ਲਬੁ ਤ ਕੂੜਾ ਨੇਹੁ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa jaa lab taa nehu kiaa lab ta koorraa nehu |
lines.english: Fareed, when there is greed, what love can there be? When there is greed, love is false.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੀਵਦਿਆ ਪੈਰਾ ਤਲੈ ਮੁਇਆ ਉਪਰਿ ਹੋਇ ॥੧੭॥
lines.translit: jeevadiaa pairaa talai mueaa upar hoe |17|
lines.english: When we are alive, it is under our feet, and when we are dead, it is above us. ||17||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਜੰਗਲੁ ਜੰਗਲੁ ਕਿਆ ਭਵਹਿ ਵਣਿ ਕੰਡਾ ਮੋੜੇਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa jangal jangal kiaa bhaveh van kanddaa morrehi |
lines.english: Fareed, why do you wander from jungle to jungle, crashing through the thorny trees?

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਿਚਰੁ ਝਤਿ ਲਘਾਈਐ ਛਪਰਿ ਤੁਟੈ ਮੇਹੁ ॥੧੮॥
lines.translit: kichar jhat laghaaeeai chhapar tuttai mehu |18|
lines.english: How long can one remain in a thatched hut which leaks when it rains? ||18||

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਸੀ ਰਬੁ ਹਿਆਲੀਐ ਜੰਗਲੁ ਕਿਆ ਢੂਢੇਹਿ ॥੧੯॥
lines.translit: vasee rab hiaaleeai jangal kiaa dtoodtehi |19|
lines.english: The Lord abides in the heart; why are you looking for Him in the jungle? ||19||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਇਨੀ ਨਿਕੀ ਜੰਘੀਐ ਥਲ ਡੂੰਗਰ ਭਵਿਓਮੑਿ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa inee nikee jangheeai thal ddoongar bhavioma |
lines.english: Fareed, with these small legs, I crossed deserts and mountains.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਜੁ ਫਰੀਦੈ ਕੂਜੜਾ ਸੈ ਕੋਹਾਂ ਥੀਓਮਿ ॥੨੦॥
lines.translit: aj fareedai koojarraa sai kohaan theeom |20|
lines.english: But today, Fareed, my water jug seems hundreds of miles away. ||20||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਰਾਤੀ ਵਡੀਆਂ ਧੁਖਿ ਧੁਖਿ ਉਠਨਿ ਪਾਸ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa raatee vaddeean dhukh dhukh utthan paas |
lines.english: Fareed, the nights are long, and my sides are aching in pain.
